assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md defines two Epics: 'Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend' (Tasks 1-3) and 'Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui' (Tasks 4-6). Two Epics created, one per repository.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 8 and 13 show Epic summaries as 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' and 'TC-9006: trustify-ui', matching the required format exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: Tasks 1-3 each have 'Parent Epic: TC-9006: trustify-backend' with Repository 'trustify-backend'. Tasks 4-6 each have 'Parent Epic: TC-9006: trustify-ui' with Repository 'trustify-ui'. Assignment is correct across all 6 tasks.

assertion_results.text: Incorporates links are created from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks)
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 17-19 explicitly state: 'Feature TC-9006 → Epic "TC-9006: trustify-backend" (Incorporates)' and 'Feature TC-9006 → Epic "TC-9006: trustify-ui" (Incorporates)', with the note: 'links go from Feature to Epics, NOT from Feature to individual Tasks'.

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are numbered 1-3, frontend tasks 4-6. Dependencies: Task 2 depends on Task 1 (2>1), Task 3 depends on Task 2 (3>2), Task 4 depends on Task 2 (4>2), Task 5 depends on Task 4 (5>4), Task 6 depends on Task 5 (6>5). All dependency ordering preserved.

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files contain: Repository (single repo name), Target Branch (main), Description, Files to Modify and/or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria (with checkboxes), and Test Requirements (with checkboxes). Verified in task-1 through task-6 files.

assertion_results.text: Epics are created with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 9 and 13 state 'Issue type: Epic (level 1), parent: TC-9006' for both Epics, matching the required level-1 issue type name and parent feature key.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1-3: Repository = 'trustify-backend' (single). Task 4-6: Repository = 'trustify-ui' (single). No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description' (substantive descriptions), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified across all task files.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:'
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 digest files (digest-task-1 through digest-task-6) contain hashes prefixed with 'sha256-md:' followed by exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. Example: digest-task-1 has 'sha256-md:48604799457d8c7473b2ed48c89233f2a8762d67aebcb282f35903bad300804e'. All hash lengths verified as 64 characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references across 6 tasks use the prescribed format. Examples: Task 1 'Applies: task modifies modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs matching the convention's .rs scope', Task 4 'Applies: task creates src/hooks/useRemediationSummary.ts matching the convention's .ts hook scope', Task 5 'Applies: task creates src/pages/RemediationDashboard/RemediationDashboard.tsx matching the convention's .tsx component scope'. No 'Not applicable' annotations found. No free-form prose rationales. All rationales reference specific files from Files to Modify/Create sections.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name
assertion_results.evidence: Feature priority is 'Major'. All 6 tasks include '## Additional Fields' with 'priority: Major'. Verified in task-1 through task-6.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature fixVersions is 'RHTPA 1.5.0', no Jira Field Defaults so scope defaults to 'both'. All 6 tasks include 'fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0' in their Additional Fields section. Verified in task-1 through task-6.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md contains a '## Field Inheritance' section (lines 22-23) stating: 'Priority: Major (propagated to all Epics and Tasks)' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated to all Epics and Tasks, fixVersion scope defaults to "both")'. Both inherited fields are documented with their propagation status.
